# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.114.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const { searchParams } = url;
  const userId = searchParams.get("userId");

  if (!userId) {
    return new Response("Missing userId parameter", { status: 400 });
  }

  const filters = Array.from(searchParams.entries())
    .filter(([key]) => key !== "userId")
    .map(([key, value]) => ({ key, value }));

  let cursor = kv.cursor(["users", userId]);
  let results = [];

  for await (const { key, value } of cursor) {
    const [, , paramKey] = key;

    if (
      filters.every(({ key: filterKey, value: filterValue }) =>
        paramKey === filterKey && value === filterValue
      )
    ) {
      results.push({ key, value });
    }
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(results), {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  });
}

serve(handler);
